commit.message:
Onboarding (#243)

* fix: accept sw_access_token cookie in backend auth middleware

The frontend Supabase login flow sets an httpOnly sw_access_token cookie
(containing the raw Supabase JWT) on the Vercel domain. When the Next.js
proxy forwards requests to the Railway backend, this cookie is included in
the Cookie header, but extractToken only checked for the backend's own
encrypted accessToken cookie.

This fix adds sw_access_token as a fallback in extractToken so that
Supabase-authenticated users can make authenticated requests through the
proxy. The raw Supabase JWT is passed to verifySupabaseToken which
validates it with the Supabase service, and resolveLocalUser then creates
or looks up the corresponding local user row.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: refactor Redis client initialization in bundler-buffer service

* fix: prefer sw_access_token cookie, add tests, fix ESM require in bundler buffer

r600: audit docs vs x130e code; update stale GPR spill and PV/PS claims

Audited SFN sources on x130e (mesa-26-debug at 568fc1deea0) against
canonical docs. Key findings:

- GPR_SPILL_DESIGN.md claimed 'DESIGN PHASE' / 'no spill fallback' but
  register_allocation_with_spill() is fully implemented and wired in at
  sfn_nir.cpp:1013. Updated status to PHASE 1 IMPLEMENTED.
- scheduler_model.md claimed spill is 'design target'. Updated to reflect
  Phase 1 implementation with Phase 2 targets listed.
- SFN_GAP_ANALYSIS.md had inconsistency: Law 1 (PV/PS) listed as PARTIAL
  in main table but FIXED in Appendix B. Updated main table to FIXED.
  Gap 2 (PV/PS scheduling-time optimization) marked RESOLVED.
- All other claims verified: KCACHE (Law 3), register constants,
  read-port validation (Law 4), Terakan SFN bridge, disk cache,
  dynamic state, terakan_sched.h Phase 0 contract.

New finding document: AUDIT_CODE_VS_DOCS_20260412.md

fix: move Config sensors from CONFIG to DIAGNOSTIC entity category

Data Sync Status, Pi API Version, and Expected API Version are
read-only status/informational sensors. EntityCategory.CONFIG is
for user-configurable settings (text inputs, selects, numbers).
These sensors were showing as unavailable/greyed in HA's device
page because CONFIG entities are treated differently by the HA UI.

Changing to DIAGNOSTIC places them in the Diagnostics section,
which matches their read-only nature and fixes the unavailable state.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- 第十二章: SR-IOV for near-bare-metal network performance (DPDK)
- 第十三章: Hotplug disks for online storage expansion
- 第十四章: Instancetype & Preference for standardised VM sizing

- 第十七章: GPU/vGPU passthrough for ML/AI workloads
- 第十八章: Systematic 4-layer troubleshooting methodology
- 第十九章: Hook Sidecar for custom VM lifecycle extensions
- 第二十章: Forklift/dedicated VMware migration tooling
- 第二十一章: Reading KubeVirt source code and contributing upstream

Story now covers the complete KubeVirt operations journey.

commit.message:
fix(providers): remove hardcoded use_oidc from azuread provider

azuread provider had use_oidc = true hardcoded, which broke local SP auth
in simulate-pipeline.sh — with no ARM_OIDC_TOKEN available locally, the
provider could not authenticate.

Fix: remove use_oidc from the provider block entirely. Auth mode is now
controlled by environment variables only:
- Pipeline (WIF/OIDC): ARM_USE_OIDC=true + ARM_OIDC_TOKEN set by pipeline steps
- Local (SP):          ARM_CLIENT_ID + ARM_CLIENT_SECRET set by simulate-pipeline.sh

The azuread provider v3 reads ARM_USE_OIDC from env automatically, so the
pipeline behaviour is unchanged. Both providers (azurerm and azuread) now
use the same env-var-driven auth pattern.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: use related tickets (not just prerequisites) for dependency grouping

The dependency grouping was only using the Prerequisites section to find
ticket-to-ticket dependencies. Most dependencies are expressed in the
Related Tickets / Related Jira Tickets sections. Updated to:

- Use union-find to cluster tickets connected by either prerequisites
  or related-ticket references within the same directory
- Detect 'Related Jira Tickets' heading (prepared format) in addition
  to 'Related Tickets' (planned format)
- Fix field name parser to strip trailing colons from bold KV pairs
  (e.g. **Title:** was captured as 'Title:' not 'Title')
- Filter out -genlog.md generation log files from ticket scanning

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Switch devcontainer to docker-in-docker for wp-env bind mounts

docker-outside-of-docker shares the host Docker socket, so bind mount
paths are resolved on the host — not inside the devcontainer. This
prevents wp-env from mounting the plugin directory into its WordPress
container, causing 'plugin could not be found' on activation.

docker-in-docker runs its own Docker daemon inside the devcontainer
where /workspaces/wp-document-revisions is a real path, so wp-env
bind mounts work correctly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Pattern D: MCP-First integration using N8N MCP Server Trigger,
  n8n-mcp-server (1598★), EspoMCP (31★), N8N2MCP (130★)
- Full comparison: Direct REST vs MCP (14 criteria decision matrix)
- Hybrid strategy: simple intents → REST, complex → MCP, events → callback
- MCP ecosystem catalog with install commands
- Cost-benefit analysis per service (N8N, CRM, RAG, Calendar)
- bot_llm.py tool-use extension sketch for Ollama tool_call → MCP
- New bot.env vars: MCP_N8N_URL, MCP_TOOL_USE_ENABLED/THRESHOLD
- Updated Phase 4 plan with MCP milestones
- 3 new decision points (#7-9) for owner

commit.message:
Fix(api): wrap custom field PUT payload

The Guesty API expects the custom fields array
wrapped in a {"customFields": [...]} object, not
sent as a bare JSON array. Also improve the error
handler in __init__.py to use err context
consistently, add exc_info for tracebacks, and
keep the user-facing error message high-level.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Grimberg <tykeal@bardicgrove.org>

commit.message:
fix(simulate-pipeline): update for platform MI and bootstrap SP changes

- Remove MI_CROSSPLANE_* variables — platform MI is now created by Terraform
  (azurerm_user_assigned_identity.platform in azure.tf) so no pre-existing MI
  needs to be supplied as input
- Rename CROSSPLANE_SP_* → BOOTSTRAP_SP_* for clarity (the SP is per-project
  and not exclusively used for Crossplane)
- Add BOOTSTRAP_SP_APP_OBJECT_ID — App Registration Object ID needed by
  TF var bootstrap_sp_app_object_id for WIF federated credential creation
- Add missing -var flags to terraform plan and destroy: aks_admin_group_object_id,
  bootstrap_sp_app_object_id, ado_pat — these override the Backstage template
  syntax placeholders that terraform.tfvars contains in skeleton/
- Fix MI_NAME to mi-{project}-platform (matches azurerm_user_assigned_identity.platform)
- Replace hardcoded MI_CROSSPLANE_CLIENT_ID validation with terraform output lookup
  (mi_client_id) so the script is not dependent on a pre-known MI client ID
- Update .env.smoketest.template to match the new variable names and remove stale MI vars

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add chapters covering the full VM operations lifecycle:
- ch7: VM 效能問題 — CPU Pinning, NUMA, virtio 設備選型
- ch8: 可觀測性 — Prometheus metrics, 凌晨 2 點告警教訓
- ch9: 快照與備份 — VirtualMachineSnapshot/Restore/Clone, 備份策略
- ch10: KubeVirt 升版 — virt-operator InstallStrategy, WorkloadUpdateController, 無感升版
- ch11: Windows Server VM — virtio-win 驅動, cloudbase-init, Sysprep 初始化

Updated 尾聲 to reflect all 11 chapters of experience.
Story grows from 394 → 570 lines.

commit.message:
🐿️ Add daily sync to 6AM checkup

The checkup was health-check only — it reported status but never synced.
If fswatch missed a change, skills wouldn't back up until next file edit.
Now the 6AM checkup runs do_sync_locked before reporting, ensuring at least
one daily backup regardless of fswatch activity.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Upgrade Node.js from 20 to 22 (LTS)

Node.js 20 reaches end-of-life April 30, 2026. Node.js 22 is the
current Maintenance LTS (supported until April 2027) and is compatible
with @wordpress/scripts.

- devcontainer.json: node feature 20 -> 22
- ci.yml: both jest and e2e jobs use Node 22, cache keys updated
- copilot-setup-steps.yml: 20 -> 22
- package.json engines: >=18 -> >=20
- README: updated version reference

commit.message:
Release v0.20.19

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add index.md generator for WG ticket directories

Add generate-indexes.py script that parses ticket .md files and generates
index.md files for each work-group directory under planned/ and prepared/.

Planned indexes group tickets by specification, then by target artifact
(repository), sorted by date with dependency ordering (prerequisites first).
Prepared indexes group tickets by specification, sorted oldest-first.

Each index includes:
- Consolidated ticket tables with key, title, status, date, and links
- Dependency groups with explanatory summaries
- Table of contents for quick navigation

Also adds a Copilot CLI skill definition (.copilot/skills/generate-indexes.md)
so the script can be re-run via the generate-indexes skill.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: MnWorkflowOrchestrator — generic real-time workflow visualization (#59)

New Maranello component for visualizing any workflow topology in
real-time with animated edges, status transitions, and phase tracking.

Features:
- 4 layout algorithms: circular, horizontal, vertical, auto (force-directed)
- Real-time status updates with smooth CSS/SVG transitions
  (idle → active → thinking → done → error)
- Animated particles on active edges
- Phase/progress indicator bar
- Per-node customization: color, icon, sublabel, badge, group
- Per-edge customization: directed arrows, labels, bidirectional
- CVA size variants (sm/md/lg/fluid)
- Accessible: role=img, aria-label, keyboard-focusable nodes
- i18n via useLocale('workflowOrchestrator')
- Zero external dependencies — pure SVG + CSS animations

Files:
- mn-workflow-orchestrator.tsx (107 lines) — main component
- mn-workflow-orchestrator.helpers.tsx (185 lines) — SVG sub-components
- mn-workflow-orchestrator.layout.ts (162 lines) — layout algorithms
- mn-workflow-orchestrator.types.ts (88 lines) — TypeScript interfaces

Also: added to catalog, barrel exports, and i18n system.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: call optimisation layer + dev panel intelligence v0.20.19

Network layer (actual runtime optimisations):
- In-flight GET deduplication via OPT_IN_FLIGHT map in tbxJiraRequest
  and tbxSnowRequest — identical parallel calls share one Promise
- Response cache with per-endpoint TTL in OPT_RESPONSE_CACHE:
  /myself=30m, /field=10m, /project=10m, /board=5m, /sprint=2m,
  default=30s. Write ops evict related cache entries.

Dev Panel intelligence extensions:
- Slow call detection (SLOW_CALL_THRESHOLD_MS=3000) with amber flag
- Rate-limit budget meter: calls/min, warns at 40, alerts at 60
- Hot Paths section: groups real network calls by triggering view
- Cache/dedup badges on log rows + hit rate % in stats bar
- Slow and Cached filter pills
- CSV export gains Source, View, Is Slow columns
- OPT_STATS reset on log clear

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix Sell to Crown pricing — banned goods pay below market

The Sell to Crown tab had its own pricing formula (separate from
procurement orders) that was still paying above market. Now:
- Banned goods: 90%/75%/60% of market at urgent/normal/low urgency
- Normal goods: modest 1.0-1.15x premium based on urgency
- Red multiplier shown when crown pays below market
- Banned goods tagged with red BANNED badge
- Description updated to explain pricing difference

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: Material-Multiplex compat shims + compiler warning fixes

- Add IccMultiplexCompat.h: shared compat macros for 7 upstream renamed
  symbols (icSigMaterial* -> icSigMultiplex*), enabling builds against
  both old cached and new upstream iccDEV
- Apply compat header to V1 (IccConformanceV5.cpp, IccConformanceHeader.cpp)
  and V2 (CfV5Checks.cpp, CfHeaderChecks.cpp)
- Fix -Wformat-nonliteral warnings in HeuristicCollector format templates
  (IccHeuristicResult.h, IccHeuristicResult.cpp) with diagnostic pragmas
- Fix -Wunused-but-set-variable warnings in IccConformanceV5.cpp
- Build: 0 warnings, 0 errors. Tests: 991/991 passing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
